gene_region.label: FMR1 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical FMR1 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: FMR1 encodes fragile X messenger ribonucleoprotein 1, an RNA-binding regulator of synaptic protein translation. This topic bundle emphasizes neurological and neurodegenerative biology, with interpretation centered on synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local FMR1 bundle is oriented toward neurological and neurodegenerative biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: FMR1 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context
Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology
Cognitive, movement-disorder, epilepsy, and psychiatric association review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: FMR1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for FMR1.
Because FMR1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use FMR1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context
Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000102081: FMR1 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: FMR1 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: FMR1: neurological and neurodegenerative biology via synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology
biorender_visuals.template_title: Microglial Activation and Neurodegeneration Linked to Aging and Inflammation
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a FMR1 neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: FMR1 neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: FMR1 neurobiology variation model is curated as a FMR1 marker for Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context, Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context
Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology
Cognitive, movement-disorder, epilepsy, and psychiatric association review
research_context: Use this marker as a FMR1 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: FMR1 neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair FMR1 neurobiology variation model with FMR1 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
